Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with [count]+ E.G.O Gifts

tips.text: Should be achieved normally by doing MDs and getting lots of gifts.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with 15+ Burn E.G.O Gifts

tips.text:
This should be doable on either normal or hard as long as you're able to get a few theme pack specific gifts. Don't forget to use fusion and keyword refresh.

WARNING: A fusion only counts as one gift, so fusing reduces your total number of gifts for that keyword. Make sure you can still reach 15 before fusing if you're aiming for this achievement. Because of this, they're not included in the list below.
If you plan to fuse Soothe the Dead, subtract 3 from your total Burn gifts count. If you plan to fuse Purloined Flame, subtract 2.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with 15+ Bleed E.G.O Gifts

tips.text:
This should be doable on either normal or hard even without the theme pack specific gifts. Don't forget to use fusion and keyword refresh.

WARNING: A fusion only counts as one gift, so fusing reduces your total number of gifts for that keyword. Make sure you can still reach 15 before fusing if you're aiming for this achievement. Because of this, they're not included in the list below.
If you plan to fuse Bloody Mist, subtract 3 from your total Bleed gifts count. If you plan to fuse Hemorrhagic Shock, subtract 2. Subtract 1 for the canto/intervallo fusions.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with 15+ Tremor E.G.O Gifts

tips.text:
This should be doable on either normal or hard as long as you're able to get a few theme pack specific gifts. Don't forget to use fusion and keyword refresh.

WARNING: A fusion only counts as one gift, so fusing reduces your total number of gifts for that keyword. Make sure you can still reach 15 before fusing if you're aiming for this achievement. Because of this, they're not included in the list below.
If you plan to fuse Coupled Oscillation, subtract 2 from your total Tremor gifts count. If you plan to fuse Omnivibro-octovecti-bell, subtract 3.Subtract 1 for the TKT fusions.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with 15+ Rupture E.G.O Gifts

tips.text:

WARNING: A fusion only counts as one gift, so fusing reduces your total number of gifts for that keyword. Make sure you can still reach 15 before fusing if you're aiming for this achievement. Because of this, they're not included in the list below.
If you plan to fuse Enrapturing Trance, subtract 2 from your total Rupture gifts count. If you plan to fuse Ruin, subtract 3. Subtract 1 for the Nocturnal Sweeping fusion.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with 15+ Sinking E.G.O Gifts

tips.text:

If you plan to fuse Black Sheet Music, subtract 2 from your total Sinking gifts count. If you plan to fuse Impending Wave, subtract 3. Subtract 1 for the canto 2 fusion.

tips.text:
This should be doable on either normal or hard as long as you're able to get a few theme pack specific gifts. Don't forget to use fusion and keyword refresh.

WARNING: A fusion only counts as one gift, so fusing reduces your total number of gifts for that keyword. Make sure you can still reach 15 before fusing if you're aiming for this achievement. Because of this, they're not included in the list below.
If you plan to fuse Lucky Pouch, subtract 3 from your total Poise gifts count. If you plan to fuse Cask Spirits, subtract 2. Subtract 1 for the canto/intervallo fusions.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with 15+ Charge E.G.O Gifts

tips.text:
This should be doable on either normal or hard as long as you're able to get a few theme pack specific gifts. Don't forget to use fusion and keyword refresh.

WARNING: A fusion only counts as one gift, so fusing reduces your total number of gifts for that keyword. Make sure you can still reach 15 before fusing if you're aiming for this achievement. Because of this, they're not included in the list below.
If you plan to fuse T-1 Perpetual Motion Machine, subtract 2 from your total Burn gifts count. If you plan to fuse T-5 Perpetual Motion Machine, subtract 3. Subtract 1 for the MotWE fusions.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with 10+ Slash E.G.O Gifts

tips.text: This should be doable on either normal or hard as long as you're able to get a few theme pack specific gifts. Don't forget to use fusion and keyword refresh.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with 10+ Pierce E.G.O Gifts

tips.text: This should be doable on either normal or hard as long as you're able to get a few theme pack specific gifts. Don't forget to use fusion and keyword refresh.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with 10+ Blunt E.G.O Gifts

tips.text: This should be doable on either normal or hard as long as you're able to get a few theme pack specific gifts. Don't forget to use fusion and keyword refresh.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with [count]+ Tier 4 E.G.O Gifts

tips.text:
Try to get as many high tier gifts as you can and fuse them to get Tier 4s. It's very unlikely that you'll be able to get the amount needed from just shops and event/boss rewards.

While you can theoretically encounter 10 or even 20 tier 4 gifts on normal difficulty, it is unlikely (or completely unrealistic for 20) for you to be able to obtain that many. Expect to have to complete this achievement on hard.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with [count]+ Fusion Recipe-only Tier 4 E.G.O Gifts

tips.text:
Try to pick theme packs that give you more chances for fusion gifts. Recommended ones are Yield My Flesh to Claim Their Bones, TimeKilling Time, Murder on the WARP Express, and Nocturnal Sweeping. Honorable mention to Miracle in District 20 BokGak.

The 10+ achievement is marked as hard only because it's unrealistic to complete it on normal.

Collection.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with 8+ Theme Pack exclusive Fusion E.G.O Gifts

tips.text: Try to pick theme packs that give you more chances for fusion gifts. Recommended ones are Yield My Flesh to Claim Their Bones, TimeKilling Time, Murder on the WARP Express, and Nocturnal Sweeping. Honorable mention to Miracle in District 20 BokGak.

Clears.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher [count] time(s)

tips.text: Just keep running MDs and you'll complete this eventually especially if you grind intervallo/bokgak currency here instead of on the event stage. Try not to burn out. You have a lot of time until the next mirror dungeon.

Clears.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher [count] time(s) - every floor has to be in Hard Difficulty

tips.text: Running MDH once a week should be enough to get you through this achievement (unless you start really late into the season). If you're clearing the other hard only achievements, you'll probably finish this way before you complete everything else.

Clears.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon up to Floor [floor] once

tips.text: Clearing up to Floor 10 means completing MD Infinity, while clearing up to Floor 15 means completing MD Extreme. For F15, if you only want this achievement and not the 50 Adversity points one, then it's pretty much the same as finishing Infinity if you don't take any Adversities, just a little harder since you have to go further and pick more Mounting Trials. Otherwise, see the 50 Adversity points achievement in the 'Adversity - EXTREME' category.

There's way too many different ways and strategies to clear MDI and MDE to actually recommend anything specific regarding team building. Just bring your strongest team and do the best you can.

If you're struggling, there's a number of tips that can help:
- Using multi-status teams can help a lot since you can activate more fusions and more gifts
- Pick theme packs that have exclusive gifts for your team's main keyword(s). The fusions are especially useful, but the rest of the gifts are also good. Remember that you can choose theme packs using starlight, so there's no reason for you to miss them.
- Buy more starlight buffs at the beginning of the run. They can make a massive difference. Most MDE runs, especially at A50, assume that you take all starlight buffs at ++ level.
- Avoid Refraction Railway theme packs unless you need a specific gift from them (e.g. {giftname:9754}} from {themepack:1109}). They add an additional +3 levels to all enemies on clear.
- Avoid difficult theme packs (e.g. {themepack:1021} and {themepack:1023} are particularly hard because you have to fight Sancho and possibly Jia Qiu) or theme packs that counter your keyword (e.g. {themepack:1106} for {status:Laceration} or {themepack:1104} for {status:Sinking}).
- Try picking the least painful Mounting Trials at the end of every floor. Remember that 3 offense levels = 1 clash power. Picking +3 offense levels is better than picking +2 clash power. Also, unless you're using {status:Sinking} or have some other ways of reducing enemy SP, always avoid coin power.
- Aim to fuse {giftname:9083} after you've gotten all the important gifts for your keyword. The increase in damage speeds up fights a lot and reduces chances for enemies to damage you. You also stagger them much faster, which makes high atk weight E.G.Os incredibly useful to stagger entire waves at higher floors.

Clears.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon up to Floor 10 10 time(s)

tips.text: See the 'Clear Mirror Dungeon up to Floor 1~10 once' achievement for tips if you're struggling with clearing MDI.

Loadout.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon Floor 5 using a Rental Team

tips.text: Self explanatory. Just clear normal or hard MD using a rental team.

Shop.text: In a single run, use the Shop Refresh function (including Keyword Refresh) [count] or more times and clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher

tips.text:
With the amount of cost you get on the higher floors on hard, this shouldn't be too difficult. Just remember that refreshes are disabled if you buy all the gifts and skill replacements in the shop.

If you're stuck in normal or struggling to get enough cost, try to do the following:
- Get starlight buffs that give you more cost or refreshes if you're struggling.
- You can buy starting gifts that give more cost or cheaper refreshes.

- Since refreshes cost more for each refresh you do in the same shop, try to even out the number of refreshes you do on each floor. The table below shows how much it would take to buy the refreshes in a shop. If you have the {giftname:9188} gift, doing 5 refreshes on each floor in MDI would cost about 158 per floor or 1580 in total, while doing 10 refreshes on each floor over 5 floors would cost 578 per floor or 2890 in total.

- Regular and Keyword refresh both count for 1 refresh, so avoid using Keyword refresh since it's expensive.
- You can aim for focused or Risky, Focused, or Abnormality encounters to get more gifts (to sell) and Cost, especially early on when they're still easier.

Shop.text: In a single run, purchase [count] or more E.G.O Gifts from Shops and clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher

tips.text:
If you're doing this on normal, you'll probably need to get the 'Star of the Beginning' and 'Binary Star-shop' starlight buffs to increase the number of gifts being sold in shops. Other than that, you mostly just need to do whatever you can to have enough Cost every time you enter a shop to buy out everything.

Pretty much any starlight buff that relates to Cost or gifts can help with this. More Cost means more gifts you can buy. Free gifts means more gifts you can sell to buy more gifts when you're short on Cost.

You can buy starting gifts that give more cost or cheaper gift purchases.

Don't be afraid to get repeat gifts from rewards if you don't need the other gifts. Except for the tier 1 vestige, the other vestiges generally sell for a decent amount.

You can aim for focused or Risky, Focused, or Abnormality encounters to get more gifts (to sell) and Cost, especially early on when they're still easier.

Shop.text: In a single run, clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher while carrying [count] or more Enhanced E.G.O Gifts (Enhancement stage does not matter)

tips.text:
This one isn't quite as demanding with the amount of cost you need compared to the other shop achievements, but you still would preferrably do whatever you can to have enough Cost every time you enter a shop.

Remember that you have to clear the MD holding the enhanced gifts. If you fuse them after enhancing, they don't count.

The enhancement tier doesn't matter, so you can just enhance as many tier 1 and 2 gifts as you can to + instead of bringing them all the way to ++.

Try to get as many gifts as you can from event rewards. You can fuse gifts that can't be enhanced to get other random gifts that could potentially be enhanced or sell them to get more cost in shops for enhancements or to buy enhanceable gifts.

Pretty much any starlight buff that relates to Cost or gifts can help with this. More Cost means more gifts you can buy. Free gifts means more gifts you can sell or fuse when you're short on Cost.

You can buy starting gifts that give more cost or cheaper enhancements.

Don't be afraid to get repeat gifts from rewards if you don't need the others. The vestiges do sell for a decent amount. You can also fuse them with unenhanceable gifts to hopefully get enhanceable ones

You can aim for focused or Risky, Focused, or Abnormality encounters to get more gifts and Cost, especially early on when they're still easier.
The following is all enhanceable gifts currently available in MD.

Shop.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher with [count] or more Cost

tips.text:
Similar to the other shop achievements, you're going to want to build up as much Cost as you can with Starlight buffs and Cost-related gifts. If you're doing this on normal, you're going to struggle a lot with the 5000 achievement, but on infinity or extreme you'll naturally get enough cost as long as you're not buying a lot from the shops trying to get the other achievements.

The same general tips from the other achievements to build up cost apply:
- Get Starlight buffs that give you more Cost or free gifts
- Buy starting gifts that give you more cost
- Don't ignore chances for duplicate gifts since you can still sell the vestiges.
- Aim for Risky, Focused, or Abnormality encounters, especially on lower floors when they're easier, to get more gifts and Cost

Shop.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher after using the Skill Search function once or more

tips.text: Use the skill search function that appears in super shops in hard mode.

Shop.text: For 1 ally, replace all of their Skill 1 and Skill 2 copies with Skill 3 in the Shop; then, clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher

tips.text:
Unlike the other shop achievements, this doesn't require too much cost unless you're really unlucky with shop refreshes. You just have to get lucky enough with the random sinner that appears in the shop skill replacement enough times in the same run. This is even easier on hard now since they introduced the skill search feature.

Replacing all Skill 1s and 2s with Skill 3s requires exactly 5 skill replacements. On normal, you can only get 1 skill replacement per shop, while on hard you can get 1 per regular shop and 2 per super shop. This means if you're aiming to do this on normal, you're going to have to hope that the same sinner appears in the shop on every single floor. That is technically possible, but don't count on it. Replacing only skill 1s is more doable on normal but it will still require some really lucky rng.

Ideally you'll refresh until you can buy all the skill replacements on the sinners you chose for this on every floor, but if you're going all the way to F10 on infinity or F15 on extreme, you can probably skip a few floors if you're really unlucky and running out of cost.

Shop.text: In a single run, use the Fusion function at least [count] times and clear Mirror Dungeon at Floor 5 or higher

tips.text:
Get as many gifts as you can, even those that don't really matter for your run. See the achievement on clearing while having up to 50 gifts for more tips on getting many gifts.

On the final floor you're aiming to do, fuse all the gifts you don't need to beat the boss. Fuse them 2 at a time to minimize the number of gifts you need and just keep fusing whatever you get with all the other gifts you don't need.

For 20 fusions, you only need at most 21 fodder gifts (including vestiges) to fuse. Less if you've already done other fusions for your team's keyword.

Combat.text: Deal [count]+ Skill damage against an enemy with a single Skill use (Shield HP dmg & dmg dealt On Hit such as Sinking Deluge or Time Moratorium counts; excludes dmg from Keywords and other sources)

tips.text: The achievement already tells you the two most straightforward ways to get this achievement: {status:SinkingSurge} and {status:TimeSuspend}.

{status:TimeSuspend}: This method is easier but slightly less predictable than the {status:SinkingSurge} method. Your goal is to apply {status:TimeSuspend} on an enemy, deal as much damage as possible, then before the next turn ends, burst all the damage dealt with another {status:TimeSuspend}. This will deal 130% of the damage you dealt as {keyword:Sloth} damage, which means you need to have dealt over 15385 damage between the first and second {status:TimeSuspend}. It's recommended that you use {status:FusionVibration} with any {status:Vibration} versions that increase damage dealt ({status:VibrationEcho}, {status:VibrationContinue}, {status:VibrationIgnition}, {status:VibrationBleeding}) and {status:VibrationExplosion}ing as many times as possible during this time. Since the enemy's HP is locked, this method isn't as restrictive with bosses as the {status:SinkingSurge} method, but it's still recommended to get {giftname:9083} to boost your damage as much as possible.

{status:SinkingSurge}: Having the enemy at 99/99 {status:Sinking} will deal 9801 {keyword:Gloom} damage, meaning you'll need an enemy that's fatal (x2) to {keyword:Gloom} or {giftname:9083}, making {status:SinkingSurge} deal 19602. The remaining damage can easily be covered by the damage from the skill itself and damage bonuses from gifts. If you're going to use this strategy, avoid bosses that change phases since most of them have mechanics that counter this (Gasharpoon or Time Ripper remove their statuses whenever they change phases, Dong Rang Who Denies All limits damage received before his phase change, Sancho ends turns early if she hits certain HP thresholds, etc). The most ideal bosses to do this on are Shock Centipede and Skin Prophet (specifically his candles) since they can't die immediately and are both fatal to {keyword:Gloom}. With {giftname:9083}, you can also do this against other bosses that don't die like You Want to Get Beat? Hurtily?.

Strong skill or AOE E.G.O: It's possible to achieve this using a strong AOE E.G.O like {ego:20307}, {ego:20608}, or {ego:21009}. Strong skills like {identity:10115}'s Furioso-Replica or {identity:10614}'s S3 can also work. You'll need a lot of damage buffing E.G.O Gifts and you'll likely have to do this at a later Extreme floor to find enemies with enough HP to survive long enough. Alternatively enemies that don't die like Skin Prophet's candles also works. The robots at the start of {themepack:1504} is a particularly good spot for this. If you've finished Canto IX, you can also use Ira to help.

Combat.text: Raise 1 enemy's Burn Potency to 99 (excluding 'Unique Burn'; in Focused Encounters, a Part)

tips.text: This will happen naturally on longer fights when you have most of the {status:Combustion} gifts.

tips.text: This will happen naturally on longer fights when you have most of the {status:Laceration} gifts.

Combat.text: Raise 1 enemy's Tremor Potency to 99 (excluding 'Unique Tremor'; in Focused Encounters, a Part)

tips.text: This will happen naturally on longer fights when you have most of the {status:Vibration} gifts.

Combat.text: Raise 1 enemy's Rupture Potency to 99 (excluding 'Unique Rupture'; in Focused Encounters, a Part)

tips.text: This will happen naturally on longer fights when you have most of the {status:Burst} gifts.

Combat.text: Raise 1 enemy's Sinking Potency to 99 (excluding 'Unique Sinking'; in Focused Encounters, a Part)

tips.text: This will happen naturally on longer fights when you have most of the {status:Sinking} gifts.
This will happen naturally on longer fights when you have most of the {status:Breath} gifts.

Combat.text: Raise 1 ally's Charge Potency to 10 (including 'Unique Charge', excluding 'Unique Charge' with fixed Potency

tips.text: This will happen naturally when you have longer fights in the later floors of MDI, but may be harder to do in normal or on lower floors. Use {status:Charge} ids that can consume large amounts of {status:Charge} at a time like {identity:10312}, {identity:10913}, {identity:10405}, {identity:11110} or {identity:10713}. If you're doing this on normal, try soloing to give that id more time to ramp up their {status:Charge} Potency.

Combat.text: Win a boss Encounter after 1 ally takes damage equivalent to 80%+ of its max HP. The said ally must survive the Encounter (applies to the last Encounter of each Floor)

tips.text: You can easily do this using any id or gift that has death protection (prevent death a single time) and letting them get hit until the death protection gets triggered. Just make sure they don't have {status:Combustion} or {status:Laceration} if they don't heal. You can also use any of the ways you can damage yourself like self-{status:Combustion}/{status:Laceration} ids or {ego:20608}.

Combat.text: Defeat all enemies in the Turn where 10 or more enemies are on the battlefield (in Focused Encounters, a Core and its connected Parts count as a single target)

tips.text: Go to {themepack:1114}. The boss stage should have a wave with 10 enemies. Just kill all of them in a turn using atk weight skills and egos.

Combat.text: Use a single Attack Skill to either Stagger or Part Break 2 or more Parts, simultaneously

tips.text: You'll need to find an abno that has multiple parts, ideally with the same HP and resists, then use an aoe. Time Ripper would be a relatively easy target for this.

Combat.text: Win an Encounter after inflicting 10 types of negative effects on a single target

tips.text: This should be relatively easy with most teams as long as enemies don't die too fast. Any debuff is considered as a separate negative effect.

Combat.text: In a single run, clear 5 Stages after activating the effects of the E.G.O Gift 'Entangled Fate' in each of them

tips.text: {giftname:9208} requires you to do a 7 A-Res. to trigger. This would be easiest to do in an Envy or Heishou team, but many other teams should be able to do it. The gift only drops from certain affinity-related theme packs.

Combat.text: Defeat the "Abnormal Seismic Zone" Theme Pack boss while carrying 5 or more liquor-related E.G.O Gifts: "Hardwood Liquor Cup," "Green Spirit," "Blood, Sweat, and Tears," "Wobbling Keg," "Reverberation," "Chalice of Trickle-down," "Downpour," "Devil's Share," "Angel's Cut," "Trash Crab Brain Wine," "Shaoxing Wine"

tips.text: Straightforward achievement. Fairly easy to do considering {themepack:1406} can appear throughout F4-10 on hard.

Combat.text: With 2 or more The Ring Identities in your Loadout, win an Encounter after having one of them gain 'Exquisite Aesthetics' with 'Exquisite Artwork' E.G.O Gift

tips.text: You need to go to {themepack:1026} on F5-10 Hard to get {giftname:9222} and {giftname:9223} which are needed to fuse this gift. To trigger {status:GreatAesthetics}, you need the Ring Ids to hit an enemy twice to give it {status:PaintingMaterial}, then have one of them kill it to gain the needed buff.

It's possible to do this on Normal by spending some starlight to get {giftname:9222} and {giftname:9223} through gift observation. You can get {giftname:9039} as well if you want to clear it immediately.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'Level Boost' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Level Boost appears on every floor from 11-15 and gives all enemies +3 levels each time if taken.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'Frailness' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Frailness appears on every floor from 11-15 and reduces the max hp of all sinners by -10% each time if taken.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'Mark of Fire' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Mark of Fire appears on floors 11 and 14 and deals fixed hp damage to all sinners at every turn end if taken. Mark of Fire on F11 I deals 5 damage on turn 1, increasing by 3 every turn up to a max of 20. Mark of Fire II on F14 deals 10 damage on turn 1, increasing by 5 every turn up to a max of 30.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'Inflation' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Inflation appears on floors 11 and 13 and increases the costs of all Shop functions (Skill Replace, Buy Gift, Heal Sinners, Enhance Gifts, and Replace Sinners). Inflation I at F11 doubles all costs. Inflation II at F13 triples all costs.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'Ego Interference' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Ego Interference appears on floors 11 and 13 and increases the costs of using E.G.Os. Ego Interference I doubles all costs and Ego Interference II triples them.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'Mental Psychosis' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Mental Psychosis appears on floors 12 and 15 and reduces the SP of all sinners at the end of every turn. Mental Psychosis I on F12 is -3SP every turn. Mental Psychosis II on F15 is -5SP every turn.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'Nerve Acceleration' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Nerve Acceleration appears on floors 12 and 15 and raises the min and max speed of all enemies. Nerve Acceleration I on F12 raises speeds by 1. Nerve Acceleration II on F15 raises speeds by 2.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'SP Fatigue' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

SP Fatigue appears on floors 12 and 15 and stops SP healing from going above a cap in battles (healing in the Shop can still reach 45SP). SP Fatigue I on F12 caps SP healing to 35. SP Fatigue II on F15 caps SP healing to 30.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'Tremor Barrier' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Tremor Barrier appears on floor 13 and removes the first Stagger Threshold on all enemies.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'Vitality Boost' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Vitality Boost appears on floor 14 and increases the max HP of all enemies by 30%.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with 1 or more 'Brutality' Adversities

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Brutality appears floor 14 and gives all enemies +2 Plus Coin Power and -2 Minus Coin Power.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Mirror Dungeon with [count] or higher Adversity Score

tips.text:
Adversities only exist on Extreme difficulty at F11-15.

Each adversity has a corresponding score attached to it. Your final adversity score is the sum of all the Adversities you took throughout your run. Originally the max adversity was 50, which made taking every Adversity on every floor a requirement to complete this achievement. Starting the April 9 update, a new adversity was added to each floor making the max 60.

The maximum Adversity Scores you can reach on each floor are as follows:
This means if you only want to partially clear this achievement, you only need to complete the corresponding floor if you pick enough Adversities (F11 for 5, F12 for 15, and so on).

If you want to complete all the way until 50, then get ready for highly difficult encounters where enemies can easily one shot you and your highest rolling EGOs are hopeless. A few things to remember:

texts: Take all the Starlight buffs at ++ if you can afford it. You'll need all the help you can get.

Bring easily accessible HP and SP healing. You might struggle to survive the longer fights without it. Gifts that help with survivability like {giftname:9754} or {giftname:9813} are also incredibly useful.

Be careful of the order in which you pick the theme packs and also which ones you decide to skip. Some have harder fights that are better done early compared to others or even skipped completely. The {themepack:1506} is particularly nasty because the Peccatulum Iraes have unavoidable counters that will stagger or outright kill your sinners if you're not resistant to them. {themepack:1509} is an unfocused encounter, making it difficult to control clashes, and the enemies have counters as well.

cells: N Corp. New League of Nine Litterateurs
Ahab Trio, Nelly, Jia Huan + Gubo
Ambling Pearl, Ardor Blossom Moth, Spiral of Contempt
You Want To Get Beat? Hurtily?, Shock Centipede, My Form Empties
WARP train and La Manchaland Bloodbags
Dawn of Green Robots (x24, unfocused battle)

texts: Try to have your entire build established by F10. You won't be able to buy a lot of things on F11-15 because of Inflation, and if you have sinners dying regularly or using a lot of EGOs, you'll end up spending most of your Cost on heals (unless you managed to pick up {giftname:9081}).
Take the level up option on events that can level you up. Pick up {giftname:9082} as well if possible. You will need all the help you can get when you're clashing on the higher floors.

Do NOT limit yourself to a single keyword. Bring a multi-status team so you can utilize more fusions and gifts, or at least use hybrid keyword identities that can make use of gifts for other keywords. Get all your main keyword(s)' gifts early and make sure you don't miss theme pack specific gifts and fusions. After that, get all the gifts you can for your damage types and secondary keywords (and Lunar Memory). They will make a big difference in how powerful your team is. Make sure not to fuse gifts of other keywords if you don't have 5+ sinners to activate them.

To add to the previous point, there are also many gifts or gift combinations that can help even when you're not using ids of that keyword. {giftname:9065} can give you bonus damage. The Sin Fragments increases the power of EGOs. {giftname:9050} can apply offense and defense level down when combined with a {status:Laceration} source like {giftname:9089}. {giftname:9024} and {giftname:9091} can apply offense and defense level down and fragile for free with {giftname:9055}. {giftname:9075} can give additional buffs with gifts like {giftname:9069}. {giftname:9070} can give a lot of free damage and offense level up with gifts like {giftname:9066}, {giftname:9063}, and {giftname:9097}. Try reading the descriptions of gifts you're not too familiar with to make sure you haven't missed anything. You can refer to the [Universal Gifts](/universal) page if you want suggestions on gifts that you can use regardless of the team you're using.

Having 30 max SP means rng can hurt you badly. Don't forget you can reset a stage when things don't go your way as long as you still have living sinners. This max is only for SP healing during a fight, which means you can still heal up to 45 SP in a shop if you want a small boost at the start of a fight.

Don't forget you have Durante abilities. Superbia isn't very useful later on since you should have more than enough EGO resources, but Pigritia, Morositas, and Ira can help in certain circumstances. Having Morositas ready for F15 is especially useful in pretty much every run.

If you're struggling on what theme packs to pick, the ones generally considered to be the easiest are {themepack:1504}, {themepack:1505}, and {themepack:1510}, with {themepack:1506} and {themepack:1509} being the hardest. Try to leave the easier ones for later.

It is recommended to get {giftname:9705} if you're planning to pick theme packs with a lot of enemies. Using a strong AOE E.G.O with your first sinner on a turn can deal a lot of damage or even stagger the entire wave if you have {giftname:9083}. Try to get it from gift observation at the start of a run since it's difficult to get it from the Hell's Chicken theme pack.

Unless you're picking a theme pack or reward that you need, maximize your theme pack refreshes to try to find The B.E. theme pack. Getting {giftname:9242} can help immensely and the other rewards are also incredibly useful if you haven't picked them up yet during the run.

Adversity - EXTREME.text: Clear Floor 15 of the Mirror Dungeon without obtaining the E.G.O Gift 'Lunar Memory'

tips.text: Pretty straightforward requirement. You don't need adversities when doing this if you want to make your life easier.

Completionist.text: Complete all Achievements (Not counting Achievements in the "Hidden" category)

tips.text: Not much to say here other than good luck!

Hidden bosses randomly appear in Extreme event nodes at a 10% chance so you can't plan for them to appear. Just do what you normally do to prepare for extreme. You should be able to deal with them especially on the lower extreme floors if you have good enough clashing and damage and if you deal with their mechanics where necessary.

Check out the encounters page for details on the boss: {enc:md|hidden-thumb}

Hidden bosses randomly appear in Extreme event nodes at a 10% chance so you can't plan for them to appear. Just do what you normally do to prepare for extreme. You should be able to deal with them especially on the lower extreme floors if you have good enough clashing and damage and if you deal with their mechanics where necessary.

Check out the encounters page for details on the boss: {enc:md|hidden-index}

Hidden bosses randomly appear in Extreme event nodes at a 10% chance so you can't plan for them to appear. Just do what you normally do to prepare for extreme. You should be able to deal with them especially on the lower extreme floors if you have good enough clashing and damage and if you deal with their mechanics where necessary.

Check out the encounters page for details on the boss: {enc:md|hidden-middle}

Hidden bosses randomly appear in Extreme event nodes at a 10% chance so you can't plan for them to appear. Just do what you normally do to prepare for extreme. You should be able to deal with them especially on the lower extreme floors if you have good enough clashing and damage and if you deal with their mechanics where necessary.

Check out the encounters page for details on the boss: {enc:md|hidden-ring}

Hidden.text: Obtain EX-Tier E.G.O Gift "A Certain Philosophy"

tips.text: You need to fuse with a 99% chance to get the keyword you want and fail it to get this gift. Just keep fusing either 3 gifts with the starlight buff to increase the fuse chance or with 4 gifts in a super shop.
You need to encounter the slot machine event and succeed in the 1% chance to get this gift.

Hidden.text: Purify a Cursed E.G.O Gift and unlock "One with the Blade" in the E.G.O Gift Compendium

tips.text: Cursed gifts appear in random event nodes, just accept the gift and clear the number of encounters stated to purify it.

Hidden.text: Purify a Cursed E.G.O Gift and unlock "Golden Hour" in the E.G.O Gift Compendium

tips.text: Cursed gifts appear in random event nodes, just accept the gift and clear the number of encounters stated to purify it.

Hidden.text: Purify a Cursed E.G.O Gift and unlock "Potentialities" in the E.G.O Gift Compendium

tips.text: Cursed gifts appear in random event nodes, just accept the gift and clear the number of encounters stated to purify it.
